input.question: How many users signed up last week?

expected.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS signup_count
FROM users
WHERE created_at >= current_date - interval '7 days'
  AND created_at < current_date;
expected.explanation: Counts the number of user records created during the previous 7 days, up to (but not including) today.

input.question: List the top 10 products by revenue this year, with their category.
input.schema_hint:
CREATE TABLE products (id INT, name TEXT, category TEXT);
CREATE TABLE order_items (id INT, product_id INT, price NUMERIC, quantity INT, ordered_at TIMESTAMP);

expected.sql:
SELECT p.name, p.category, SUM(oi.price * oi.quantity) AS revenue
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id
WHERE oi.ordered_at >= date_trunc('year', current_date)
GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.category
ORDER BY revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
expected.explanation: Aggregates revenue per product for the current calendar year, joins to get the category, and returns the top 10 sorted highest-first.
Permanently removes the `old_logs` table and all its data.
warnings: This query is destructive — it will permanently delete the table and ALL rows in it. Confirm there are no dependent foreign keys or views before running.
Consider `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS old_logs;` if you want the command to succeed even when the table is already gone.
